profile: Multi-party (M-of-N / ordered) human approval over EP-SIGNOFF-v1 members
description: Adversarial conformance vectors for EP multi-party quorum approval. Each member is a real Class-A WebAuthn assertion; the quorum predicate (all-signatures-valid, action-binding, distinct-humans, distinct-keys, roles-admitted, threshold, order, chain-linked, window) is fail-closed. Ordered quorums chain each signoff to its predecessor (prev_context_hash) so order is proven cryptographically, not by timestamps. A conformant verifier MUST return expect.valid for every vector.

vectors.description: Ordered quorum (strong chain): Program Officer -> Authorizing Official -> Inspector General, distinct humans, each signoff cryptographically chained to its predecessor, all bound to the action
Ordered prefix quorum: the first 2 of 3 roster slots approve in order with a signed predecessor chain
Threshold quorum: 2 of 3 eligible approvers sign the exact action
An unknown policy mode must not be silently interpreted as threshold
One quorum member signed in a cross-origin WebAuthn ceremony
Only 2 of a required 3 approvers signed
The Program Officer also signs the Inspector General slot — one human, two slots
Ordered quorum signed out of sequence (times not strictly increasing in role order)
One approver signed a different action_hash than the quorum authorizes
Signatures span longer than the policy window_sec
One member assertion verifies against the wrong key
A signer whose (role, approver) is not an eligible quorum slot
Strong-chain ordered quorum where the final signoff commits to the wrong predecessor hash — chain_linked fails though every signature is valid
Two distinct approver identities sign with the same device key — distinct_keys fails
The action initiator is also a counted approver — one party both initiates and approves (SoD violation)
distinct_humans:false with one device key across two seats — distinct_keys is unconditional and must still reject
